Knowledge Management

Operationalizing the Regional Assessment

inSileco

2026-04-14

Let us start at the end

What might the outputs of the assessment look like?

NCEA

Open with the end state rather than with methods. Use this as a visual anchor: this is the kind of regional output people can work from when the assessment is structured to be useful in practice.

What questions does this answer?

The current state of the region

Risks to assessment priorities

Pathways of effects

Management & mitigation priorities

Then what?

Single set of answers

Assessment quickly becomes outdated

Accumulated knowledge is scattered

What if?

Single set of answers

Can be updated and reused

Value grows as knowledge accumulates

CEA Explorer

Where does this start from?

Interoperable metadata


Which is simply properly planned and

executed knowledge management

Knowledge Management

Organize the knowledge

Clear terms, clear categories, clear sources.

Document it well

People can understand what it means and where it came from.

Connect the information

Updates in one place can support many uses.

Build tools on top

Maps, summaries, and decision support tools become easier to maintain.

This is the light reverse-engineering slide. Avoid technical jargon. The message is simple: organized and connected knowledge makes updateable tools possible.

What does this look like?

Can be as simple as a shared google sheets

ERSL Google sheet

What do we do with it?

Knowledge Hubs

ERSL Hub

What does this make possible?

Find & Connect

Update & Reuse

Flexibility

This is the missing bridge slide. The hub is not just a place to store information. It makes the knowledge easier to find, connect, update, and reuse across discussions and assessments.

Why does this matter?

Shared regional memory

Updatable context

Reuse across assessments

Operational capacity

In a nutshell

Levarages the Regional Assessment

to build capacity beyond the report

When the next project arrives

Build on existing context

Assess risk (much!) faster

Support defensible decisions

Project builder

A well-structured assessment is not just an answer

It is an operational system for managing risk over time

And it begins with proper knowledge management

Thank you!
